# General information

## Medical Device Regulation (MDR)

We do not generate, interpret, process and mutate medical data, We simply display it. This means we are not a risk class two medical device and we do not have to comply with the MDR.

However, we plan to be ISO 13485 certified and future software might become a risk class two medical device. In that case, we will make sure to comply with the MDR.

# 2019-08 Insufficient control of suppliers

risk# Risk

| **Threat**

A supplier does not meet (contractual) requirements or service level agreement, ISO 27001 certification expires, EU-US Privacy shield compliance is discontinued.

 |
| **Origin**

Insufficient control of suppliers creates a critical supply chain security risk where your organization becomes vulnerable through third-party components you don't directly manage.

**Delayed vulnerability awareness** means you may not know about security flaws until it's too late. Suppliers don't always promptly notify customers about vulnerabilities, and without proper supplier monitoring, critical patches might be missed or applied too slowly.

**Cascading compromise potential** exists because attackers often target widely-used supplier components to maximize their impact. One exploited vulnerability in a popular framework or library can simultaneously affect thousands of organizations that use it.

**Limited incident response control** hampers your ability to respond quickly to threats. When a supplier component is compromised, you're dependent on the supplier's timeline for fixes and may have little visibility into the scope or nature of the vulnerability.

 |
| **Category**

ConfidentialityBlueAvailabilityYellow

 |
| **Owner**

Management in collaboration with Security Officers

 |

# 2019-05 Security vulnerability in underlying technology is exploited

risk# Risk

| **Threat**

A security vulnerability in underlying technology, such as web/database server or used frameworks/libraries, is exploited.

 |
| **Origin**

This risk represents a **foundational security failure** where attackers exploit weaknesses in the core technologies your applications depend on, rather than targeting your custom application code directly.

**Infrastructure-level compromise** occurs because underlying technologies like web servers (Apache, IIS, Nginx), database servers (MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQL Server), and frameworks (React, Angular, .NET) form the foundation of your entire application stack. A vulnerability at this level can provide attackers with deep system access that bypasses application-level security controls.

**Widespread exposure** is particularly dangerous because these technologies are typically used across multiple applications and systems within an organization. A single exploited vulnerability in your web server could potentially compromise every web application running on that platform.

**Privileged access acquisition** often results from these exploits because underlying technologies frequently run with elevated system privileges. Successful exploitation can give attackers administrative access, database permissions, or the ability to execute system-level commands.

**Detection evasion** is easier for attackers because vulnerabilities in underlying technologies may not trigger application-level monitoring and logging systems. The malicious activity occurs below the application layer where many security tools focus their attention.

**Lateral movement facilitation** happens when compromised underlying technologies provide attackers with a foothold to explore and attack other connected systems, databases, or network resources that trust the compromised component.

**Legacy and patching challenges** compound the risk because underlying technologies are often more difficult to update than application code. They may require system downtime, extensive testing, or coordination across multiple teams, leading to delays in applying critical security patches.

**Attack tool availability** makes exploitation more likely since vulnerabilities in popular technologies like Apache Struts, Microsoft IIS, or common JavaScript frameworks often have readily available exploit code that attackers can easily deploy.

This type of vulnerability is particularly insidious because it undermines the security foundation that all your other protective measures depend on.

 |
| **Category**

ConfidentialityBlue IntegrityGreenAvailabilityYellow

| **Owner**

# Summary

The information retention policy defines the retention periods for all information in our organization.

# Principles

Refer to the table below for relevant retention periods and disposal instructions for all data types:

INLINE

| Data type | Retention period | Location | Disposal instructions |  |
| Applicant data | 1 year after application, only if the applicant agrees to be kept on file | Digitally on a file server | Delete |  |
| Personnel records | 5 years after termination | Microsoft OneDrive | Delete |  |
| MedMij patient access and event logs | 36 months after access or event | Within the PGO-database under encryption and only accessible by patients. | Delete |  |
| Customer records | 7 years after the last transaction | Digitally in CRM system | Delete |  |
| Source code | Unlimited | Azure DevOps, older commits are archived in a read-only GitLab space/backup | Archive |  |
| Email | Unlimited, unless it contains any of the data types above | Digitally on the mail servers | Delete |  |

# Responsibilities

- The  is responsible to keep this policy up to date
- Management will approve the policy after significant changes
- The policy is applicable to all internal and external personnel

# Testing policy

[Azure wiki page](https://dev.azure.com/medrecord/MEDrecord%20ISMS/_wiki/wikis/MEDrecord-ISMS.wiki/1181/Testing-policy)

# Summary

The testing policy describes rules and principles on how testing is applied in MEDrecord company.

# Principles

INLINE## Features

All user stories must contain a test plan for development and acceptance

Features must not be accepted when tests fail

Code reviews should only take place when:

All tests pass

The test plan satisfies requirements

## Testing strategy

The QA tester is responsible for the testing strategy and keeping this up-to-date, taking the following into account:

Types of tests to be performed

Schedules for tests

Tools

Static analysis

Unit testing

UI testing

Integration testing

Penetration testing

Test regression

Automated testing where possible, with a focus on **security and reliability**

**Unit tests must be written where relevant** to ensure code correctness

**Testing must be performed in DEV, TEST, and ACCEPTANCE environments** before a feature is released to production

Test cases written for every feature and tests documented internally for future reference

# Responsibilities

The lead developer is responsible to keep this policy up to date

Management will approve all changes

The policy applies to all developers, internal and external, and outsourced development

# Related policies

Information security policy

Secure development policy

trueINLINE## An authorization request is made

The organizational unit team leader/HR creates a task according to the [authorization request task template](https://dev.azure.com/knowl/ISMS%20security%20and%20HRM/_wiki/wikis/ISMS-security-and-HRM.wiki/1234/Authorization-request-template) within their own organizational unit Azure group.

## Approval of management

The manager will:

Verify the authenticity of the request by double-checking the sender and requested access rights.

If the request is in line with the **:** access control policy

Check if the request poses a threat to the requirements as set in[ A.6.1.2 Segregation of duties](https://www.google.com/url?q=https%3A%2F%2Fmedrecord.atlassian.net%2Fwiki%2Fspaces%2FISMS%2Fpages%2F491999%2FA.6.1.2%2BSegregation%2Bof%2Bduties&sa=D&sntz=1&usg=AFQjCNF2EPpKgJ0nBgYc3dB-1sCOkvrqSg).

Check-in case of an Administrator access request: Does the requester have the proper competences?

## The authorization request is carried out

Management/DevOps will create accounts and assign the requested access rights.

## Archiving

The Authorisation Request task is automatically retained by the Azure environment. The entire process is logged through default Azure task and checklist functionality.

# Password policy

[Azure wiki page](https://dev.azure.com/medrecord/MEDrecord%20ISMS/_wiki/wikis/MEDrecord-ISMS.wiki/1177/Password-policy)

# Summary

The password policy defines requirements for passwords.

# Principles

INLINE
- Passwords should be strong (at least 8 characters, usage of lowercase/uppercase/numbers/symbols)
- Do not use the same password for more than one service or system
- Change the password at least twice per year
- Do not use variants of the old password (e.g. adding a number to the old password)
- On account creation on most services a password reset link is sent, in other situations a temporary password is sent through email. This temporary password is to be changed at first login.

## Password Manager

As it is almost humanly impossible to remember all the different strong passwords, we bought an enterprise-level password manager (LastPass Enterprise). We recommend all employees to use the password manager for their company accounts.

We recommend using the LastPass password generator for account passwords.

# Responsibilities

- The [Information security officer](https://idtect.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ISMS/pages/3964959/Information+security+officer) is responsible to keep this policy up to date
- Management will approve the policy after significant changes
- The policy is applicable to all internal and external personnel

# Information security policy

# Summary

We recognize the security and privacy value of personal health information and have therefore implemented an information security management system with additional controls of NEN 7510 to control all our efforts towards information security. The scope of this value is broadened by participation in the MedMij network and for this reason we have implemented extra controls as defined in the [MedMij framework](https://afsprakenstelsel.medmij.nl/).

[Azure wiki page](https://dev.azure.com/medrecord/MEDrecord%20ISMS/_wiki/wikis/MEDrecord-ISMS.wiki/1054/Information-security-policy)

# Policy statements

INLINE**Designation of Responsibility:**

The responsibility for overseeing all information security efforts rests with the Information Security Officer

**Documentation and Accessibility:**

Policies and procedures documents will be diligently maintained, updated, and readily accessible to all relevant stakeholders and employees. All the documentation is being kept inside the Azure Wiki and Confluence. In a later stage, we will move the policies and procedures to the FynTun platform which will be accessible for all the employees.

**Training and Awareness:**

Information security awareness training sessions will be conducted regularly to ensure all employees are equipped with the necessary knowledge and skills to mitigate security risks effectively. See more details [here](https://dev.azure.com/medrecord/MEDrecord%20ISMS/_wiki/wikis/MEDrecord-ISMS.wiki/1260/Security-training-and-awareness-policy).

**Implementation of Measures:**

Organizational and technical measures will be implemented to safeguard our information assets against unauthorized access, disclosure, alteration, and destruction.

**Deviation and Incident Management:**

Robust procedures will be established to promptly address and rectify any deviations from security protocols and to manage security incidents effectively.

**Compliance with Laws and Regulations:**

We are committed to full compliance with all relevant laws and regulations within our jurisdiction concerning information security and data protection.

**Continuous Improvement:**

To uphold our commitment to excellence, we continually review and define new security objectives to enhance our information security posture.

**Seeking Compliance to ISO 27001:**

In our pursuit of excellence and to provide assurance to our stakeholders, we strive for compliance with ISO 27001 standards.

# Responsibilities

The Information security officer is responsible for keeping the policy up to date

Management will approve the policy after any major update

The Information security policy applies to all stakeholders of our organization

## 1. Create a page

- Click "Create" and select "Blank Page" to create your first page.
- New pages are created as children of the page you are currently viewing.

## 2. Add to your page

- Click "Edit" to enter the Confluence editor and use the page layouts feature to structure your content using sections and columns.
- Use headings to format your text and drag and drop images into your page to provide visual interest.
- Click "Insert" and select "Other Macros" to add macros for navigation, special formatting and other media.

**On this page:**

## 3. Organize your pages

Here are some tips for organizing your content.

#### Change the page order

The sidebar on the left displays your pages in a hierarchy. If you have Space Administrator permissions you can click "Space Tools" > "Reorder Pages" to move pages around.

#### Add labels

Labels help keep pages organized and make it easier for you to find the information you need. Click "Labels" at the bottom of a page to add or edit. The "Related pages" section on this page uses labels too!

#### Make templates

Standardise and speed up the page creation process with templates. You can create and format a template with page layouts, standard headings and instructional text for hints and guidelines. Check out our sample page on ""

# Making a template

Give your authors a helping hand by using templates in your documentation space. You will need Space Administrator permissions to create templates.

To create a template:

- Go to "Space Tools" in the sidebar, select "Content Tools" and create a new template.
- Click "Page Layout" and add sections and columns to your page.
- Add headings and sub-headings as needed.
- Choose "Instructional Text" from the "Template" menu and add text that is only visible in the editor.
- Save your template.

Your template will become available in the Create dialog for this space.
